name: Janice Okun's Buffalo Wings
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut off and discard the small tip of each wing.
2. Cut the main wing bone and second wing bone at the joint.
3. Sprinkle the wings with salt, if desired, and pepper to taste.
4. Heat the oil in a deep-fat fryer or large casserole until it reaches about 375°F (190°C).
5. Add half of the wings to the hot oil and cook for about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until they are golden brown and crisp. Remove them with a slotted spoon and drain well.
6. Add the remaining wings to the fryer and cook for another 10 minutes or until they are also golden brown and crisp.
7. Drain the cooked wings on paper towels.
8. Melt the butter in a saucepan over medium heat.
9. Stir in 2 to 5 tablespoons of hot sauce and vinegar until well combined.
10. Place the wings on a warm serving platter and pour the butter mixture over them.
11. Serve with blue cheese dressing and celery sticks.
